english: Pizza with Arugula and Fresh Mozzarella

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002682). No specific rucola-mozzarella pizza subclass exists; broader match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific rucola-mozzarella variant code exists; broader category match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for rucola mozzarella pizza; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one to two slices of a personal-sized rucola mozzarella pizza, approximately 150 g, as served in a casual Italian or Western restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — Rucola Mozzarella (G00010715)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010715 for Rucola Mozzarella. The HPB record reports protein at 0.0 g/100g and dietary fibre at 0.0 g/100g, which is atypical for a full pizza dish and likely reflects the dressing or glaze component only rather than the complete assembled dish. Values are preserved as reported; see notes for clinical context. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Pizza, plain baked dough (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for rucola mozzarella pizza in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 61 is estimated from plain pizza dough / cheese pizza analogues reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), which lists pizza, plain baked as GI ~60–80 depending on topping and dough type. A midpoint of 61 is used as a conservative estimate for a thin-crust vegetable-topped pizza. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 15.54) / 100 ≈ 9.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100g) with negligible potassium (5 mg/100g) and zero dietary fibre; sodium is low at 3 mg/100g per HPB record but the full assembled dish with mozzarella cheese will carry substantially higher sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that the HPB nutrient profile for this item likely reflects only the dressing or glaze component; the complete pizza dish with mozzarella will contribute significantly more sodium, and the high sugar load (9.94 g/100g) independently elevates cardiovascular risk per AHA 2018 guidance — request preparation without balsamic glaze or honey and limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 9.94 g/100g with zero dietary fibre indicates rapid glucose absorption with no fibre-mediated modulation; estimated GI of 61 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 9.5 per 150 g serving represent a meaningful glucose challenge
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this dish as recorded due to the high free-sugar content and absence of dietary fibre; if consuming the full pizza dish, pairing with a salad containing non-starchy vegetables and requesting omission of balsamic glaze will reduce the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content (9.94 g/100g) with negligible fibre and potassium; saturated fat from mozzarella cheese in the full dish is not captured in the HPB record but is clinically relevant
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, the high sugar content of this preparation is the primary concern as excess free sugar raises triglycerides and promotes endothelial inflammation; the full assembled pizza with mozzarella will also contribute saturated fat, so portion control and substitution of balsamic glaze with olive oil are recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rucola Mozzarella Pizza is an Italian-style pizza topped with fresh mozzarella cheese, cherry tomatoes, and fresh rucola (also known as rocket or arugula), typically served in casual Italian and Western dining restaurants in Singapore and internationally. The pizza base is a thin, baked wheat-flour dough, and the dish is assembled with tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and cherry tomatoes before baking, with raw rucola added after baking to preserve its peppery, slightly bitter flavour and delicate texture. The mozzarella provides a creamy, mild, milky character that contrasts with the sharp, peppery bite of the rucola. The HPB Singapore nutrient record for this item (G00010715) reports an unusually low protein value of 0.0 g/100g and zero dietary fibre, which is inconsistent with a fully assembled pizza containing mozzarella and vegetable toppings; this profile likely reflects the dressing or balsamic glaze component only. Clinically, the high sugar content (9.94 g/100g) with zero fibre is the dominant concern for Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension management. The estimated glycemic index of 61 (medium) and glycemic load of approximately 9.5 per 150 g serving indicate a moderate-to-high glucose challenge. Rucola is a source of vitamin K, folate, and glucosinolates with potential anti-inflammatory properties, but these benefits are not captured in the current nutrient profile. This dish is vegetarian and contains gluten and dairy allergens.

confidence_reason: The HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010715 is an approved primary source for Singapore food records, giving high confidence in source provenance. However, the nutrient profile (0.0 g protein, 0.0 g dietary fibre, 9.94 g sugar, 0.25 g fat per 100g) is inconsistent with a fully assembled rucola mozzarella pizza and likely reflects only the dressing or glaze component. This internal inconsistency reduces confidence to medium. GI is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement.
notes: ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): protein_g is reported as 0.0 g/100g per HPB record G00010715. This is confirmed zero per the HPB source but is nutritionally anomalous for a pizza dish containing mozzarella cheese; the HPB record likely captures only the dressing or balsamic glaze component rather than the full assembled dish. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source G00010715, again consistent with a glaze/dressing profile rather than a complete pizza. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source G00010715. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source G00010715, which is also anomalous for a dish containing mozzarella. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB record G00010715 and not available in the Singapore HPB Foodo database for this item as of 2026-04-17. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB record G00010715. DATA QUALITY FLAG: The nutrient profile in HPB G00010715 is internally inconsistent with a fully assembled pizza dish. The values (42 kcal/100g, 0g protein, 0.25g fat, 10.36g carbohydrate, 9.94g sugar, 3mg sodium) are characteristic of a balsamic glaze or sweetened dressing rather than a complete pizza. This record should be flagged for re-measurement or re-classification in the source database. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_00002682 and FoodEx2 A00LC are broader-category matches; no specific rucola-mozzarella pizza subclass exists in either ontology as of 2026-04-17.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Identified that input per_100g_meta used HPB as source — confirmed HPB is an approved primary source. Noted nutrient profile anomaly (0g protein, 0g fibre, high sugar) and documented in notes.
Iteration 1: Added zero-macro justification for protein_g, dietary_fibre_g, magnesium_mg, cholesterol_mg per g4 rule.
Iteration 1: Added -1 sentinel documentation for all unreported micronutrients and fatty acid fractions per g3 rule.
Iteration 1: Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 with full documentation.
Iteration 1: Added all three required health_context conditions with clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array — all strings present verbatim.
Iteration 2: Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance — Western/Italian dish does not require Singaporean multilingual names. local_names left null with no G6 violation.
Iteration 2: Confirmed ontology codes are defensible broader-category matches, not invented.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' as per validator feedback to align with allowed values.
